Title: Family reunited after 95 years across Malaysia–Indonesia border
Article:
JERANTUT: An emotional and heart-warming story unfolded when a family separated for almost 95 years finally tracked down their relatives in Dumai, Indonesia, after decades of searching.
Family member Rosmi Jamat, 64, a retired teacher from Bandar Jengka, said her late father, Jamat Jaikaran, often advised his nine children to try to find relatives left behind in Pacitan, East Java.
Rosmi said her father, who was 18 at the time, migrated to Malaya in 1935 due to a malaria epidemic in his hometown.
"My late father boarded a ship and stopped in Singapore before continuing his journey to Port Dickson to start a new life in Linggi, Malaya," she said.
Efforts to trace the family had continued with his siblings, now aged between 59 and 79, for more than two decades, but the search often reached a dead end.
A breakthrough came in mid-2025 when an acquaintance, Nor Azli Azrami, 59, from Port Dickson, met two migrants from Pacitan, Supardi and Tukiyat, who were then in Linggi before returning to their hometown.
"We shared stories about Kampung Pacitan and asked for their help to trace my late father's siblings who were still in Indonesia.
[[nid:1463134]]
"As a result of their help, we obtained information on several relatives who still have blood ties to our family," he said.
Rosmi said after obtaining phone numbers and contacting relatives in Indonesia, the information shared matched her late father's account.
They were also informed that one of her late father's younger siblings had migrated to Dumai in 1981 and settled there with his family.
Through these efforts, the family eventually traced five cousins, namely Parlan Jumangin, 65, Rokayah, 60, Jahri, 51, Purwati, 51, and Muji, 48, children of her late father's younger sibling, Jumangin.
Rosmi said the Malaysian group was taken to visit the relatives' homes in Dumai to reconnect family ties that had been severed for several generations.
The reunion became even more emotional when another of her late father's younger siblings, who had migrated to Bengkalis, also attended the historic meeting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreigners caught running illegal transport services with rented cars in Terengganu - RTD
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has detected the activities of foreigners in the state who use rented vehicles to carry passengers and transport goods.
Terengganu RTD director Mohd Zamri Samion said investigations found that most of the individuals detained involved Rohingya ethnic groups who offered the service using private vehicles rented from locals.
He said the activity not only violated the law, but also endangered the lives of other road users as the drivers involved did not have a competent driving licence and a motor vehicle licence.
"We have detected the activities of foreigners, especially Rohingya, who use private vehicles rented by locals to carry passengers, transport children and carry various tasks in their daily activities.
"This matter cannot be taken lightly because most of them do not have a competent driving licence and a motor vehicle licence. Let's take the recent case of a Rohingya man who drove dangerously, causing the death of a motorcyclist," he said at a press conference at Terengganu State RTD office here, today.
Mohd Zamri said Terengganu RTD had seized 114 vehicles and issued 559 summonses for various offences as a result of the Foreigner Enforcement Operation (Ops PEWA) carried out since June 1 until today.
He said 93 of the vehicles seized involved Rohingya ethnic drivers who did not have any driving qualifications or valid vehicle ownership documents.
"We want to send a clear message that strict action will be taken against foreign drivers who break the law, and even against vehicle owners who are found to be in cahoots with vehicle rental companies," he said.
Mohd Zamri said RTD also carried out undercover activities and entered villages to detect foreigners who drive vehicles without valid licenses and documents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Switzerland votes on proposal to cap population at 10mil
Article:
ZURICH: Switzerland voted on Sunday on whether to back a proposal to cap the country's population in a referendum likened to Britain's Brexit vote, which could have far-reaching consequences for the economy and Bern's relations with the European Union.
Driven by concern about immigration, pressure on public services and housing, the constitutional change pitched by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, or SVP, would mandate that the population must not exceed 10 million by 2050. Official projections put it on track to do so by the early 2040s.
The unprecedented scheme to dictate population levels by law sits alongside burgeoning efforts by the political right in Europe to set tougher curbs on immigration, fed by discontent about the cost of living, weak economic growth and crime.
"If it goes above 10 million, it will become tight, and immigration should be restricted," said Helen Gulea, a 58-year-old seamstress and part-time kiosk worker in Zurich, originally from Kenya, who voted by post for the cap.
Results of the vote are expected to begin coming in from around midday (1000 GMT).
If the proposal were adopted, reaching the 10 million mark would trigger a process that could make Switzerland scrap its free movement of labour agreement with the EU, whose member states supply much of the Alpine country's workforce.
Switzerland's ageing population is already above 9 million and polls suggest public opinion is finely balanced.
A final survey this month showed opinion turning against the proposal. An earlier poll had suggested it could pass.
Patrick Leisibach, a migration expert at think-tank Avenir Suisse, said concern was now widespread that overcrowding was stretching public infrastructure to the limit.
"There's a traditional anti-immigration vote on the right wing, but these days even many on the left are feeling the pressure," he said.
SINGLE MARKET ACCESS
Under its system of direct democracy, the Swiss electorate generally votes four times a year on national referendums, which also require the support of the majority of its cantons to pass.
The government and parliament have urged voters to reject the SVP's so-called "sustainability initiative" as folly at a delicate time for Switzerland's export-oriented economy.
Johanna Alves, a 33-year-old Swiss student, voted against it, saying she worked in an international environment which, she worried, could be in jeopardy if the proposal passes.
"And I think it will also be really terrible for the Swiss economy in general, for our healthcare system, for research, for all of it," she said.
Last year, President Donald Trump slapped the highest U.S. tariffs in Europe on Swiss goods, and the prospect of a population curb could complicate corporate planning.
But SVP lawmaker Thomas Matter argued Swiss gains in prosperity had not kept pace with overall immigration and the country needed to step on the brakes.
Weeks before Trump returned to power, Switzerland sealed a deal with Brussels to deepen economic integration with the EU.
That, and other agreements governing bilateral trade relations, could be cast into doubt by a population cap with free movement a pillar of the EU single market.
Swiss voters have often rejected measures deemed harmful to the long-term interests of the economy, but that tendency has become less predictable.
In 2014, the voters bucked expectations by narrowly passing an SVP-backed proposal to curb EU immigration. Still, its impact was later diluted in the legislative process.
Should the current initiative pass, parts of it, including the prospect of Switzerland terminating free movement with the EU, could face their own referendums, officials say in private.
Even some SVP figures say the proposal is not meant to stop free movement, but to serve as a wake-up call.
"I don't want freedom of movement ended," said Heinz Taennler, an SVP politician and finance director of the canton of Zug. "Another million people can still immigrate to Switzerland, but the government needs to take action."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersama targets 50pct women, 70pct under-50 candidates, Rafizi says
Article:
SEREMBAN: Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) is targeting 50 per cent women candidates and 70 per cent candidates aged 50 and below in any election it contests, including the upcoming Negri Sembilan election.
President Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said the party's decision to contest independently, without joining any coalition, had made the candidate selection process more straightforward.
He said Bersama was still accepting applications from individuals interested in becoming candidates for the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections through an online platform.
"We will do our best to field as many women candidates as possible. We just need qualified women candidates from the Malay, Chinese and Indian communities to come forward and become our candidates," he said at the Jelajah Kancil Negri Sembilan event last night.
"This is what we are discussing — we want 70 per cent of Bersama's candidates to be under 50 years old," he said.
Rafizi, a former economy minister, said the party's independence from any coalition had made candidate selection easier as there were no seat-sharing negotiations.
He said candidates would be matched with suitable seats, noting that some constituencies were more suited to different voter groups.
"To older candidates, don't be upset with me because there is still a 30 per cent allocation, which is quite significant — especially if Bersama contests all seats," he said.
Rafizi also said about 25,000 members had registered with Bersama since its launch three weeks ago, describing them as "crazy" for joining a party with no big names in Malaysian politics.
"I never actually thought anyone would want to join Bersama when we launched three weeks ago, because I felt only crazy people would join this party.
"Because this party contests against all parties and doesn't even have big names. It looks like a party of crazy people," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australia tribunal rejects move by Japan's Inpex to stop LNG strike
Article:
PERTH: An Australian labour tribunal on Sunday rejected an application by Japanese gas company Inpex to halt a strike by some 400 oil and gas workers at its Ichthys LNG project.
The Fair Work Commission (FWC) rejected Inpex's claim that a shutdown would hurt the Australian economy due to lost export revenue and would risk dangerous blackouts.
The strike at the 9.3-million-ton-per-year facility will now run until June 23 with a ban on the loading of all cargoes. The commission's deputy president, Michael Easton, ruled that unions and the company must keep bargaining. Bill Townsend, Inpex's corporate senior vice president, called the result "disappointing" and confirmed an updated offer for employees was being prepared.
"Reaching an equitable and sustainable agreement with employees is a priority," he said.
"We are planning accordingly to mitigate impacts while maintaining safe and stable operations."
NO EVIDENCE OF HARM
After a lengthy hearing that began on Saturday, Easton said he had found no evidence of an adverse economic effect from a strike or a danger to Australia's Northern Territory but accepted Inpex's view that there could be a halt to production that could last up to a week.
"I do not regard this to be a significant disruption. At least some of the previous production will not be lost as soon as the loading ban is lifted," he said.
Power and Water Corp, a government-owned utility for the Northern Territory, has organised contingency measures to avoid blackouts, Easton said, while the Ichthys liquefied natural gas facility has sustained more comprehensive outages in the past with no adverse effect.
STRIKE ESCALATION AND EASING
Strikes escalated on Thursday to periods of up to eight hours after Inpex and union groups failed to find a solution. Late on Friday, the strike periods were wound back to two blocks of two hours at the beginning and end of a shift.
The Offshore Alliance, made up of the Australian Workers Union (AWU) and the Maritime Union of Australia, had led bargaining with Inpex since last year and first threatened strike action in April. The Electrical Trades Union is also part of the strike, which seeks better pay, career progression and job security.
During Saturday's hearing, both sides said substantial progress had been made since FWC-mediated talks started, but there were some outstanding matters.
On Sunday, AWU's Western Australia branch secretary and Offshore Alliance spokesperson Brad Gandy said his workers were some of the most qualified in the country and were essential during an energy crisis.
"This is exactly why the workers who keep the facilities running deserve to have security in their employment and be paid accordingly," he said.
LNG CARGOES HIT
The strike will result in LNG and condensate storage onshore reaching capacity within a few days, forcing a production shutdown, Inpex's superintendent for onshore, Damien Chandler, told the commission.
If production is halted for a week, four LNG cargoes would miss their loadings, an Inpex employee said, while two condensate cargoes had already missed loading.
Inpex has also argued outages should not occur during periods of global energy insecurity given that Australia has secured agreements for continued supply of liquid fuels based on its energy relationships in Asia.
Asian spot LNG prices are up 75 per cent since the US-Israeli war on Iran began and were recently at US$18.80 per million British thermal units.
Another Inpex employee said offshore production could slow and that this could create technical challenges that would also lead to a shutdown.
Ichthys is a joint venture between Inpex, France's TotalEnergies and the Australian subsidiaries of CPC Corporation Taiwan, Osaka Gas, Kansai Electric Power , JERA and Toho Gas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police probe theft of laptop bag from Setapak kiosk, suspect sought
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating the theft of a laptop bag containing cash, personal belongings and a laptop from a UniFi kiosk at Setapak Sentral in Setapak.
Wangsa Maju police chief Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman said a police report was lodged on June 11 by the victim, who said the bag had been left on a chair at his workplace.
He said investigations and a review of closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage showed an unidentified person had taken the bag before leaving the premises.
The stolen bag contained personal items, cash and a laptop, he said in a statement today.
Annas said the case is being investigated under Section 380 of the Penal Code for theft in a building, which carries a punishment of up to 10 years' imprisonment and a fine upon conviction.
He urged anyone with information to contact the Wangsa Maju district police headquarters at 03-9289 9222 or investigating officer Sergeant Mat Rosli at 010-972 2616.
Police are also continuing efforts to identify and trace the suspect captured in the CCTV footage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two unrelated missing persons found drowned in Sungai Kedah, 100m apart
Article:
ALOR SETAR: Two persons, including a teenager with a learning disability who had been reported missing since last Friday, were found drowned in Sungai Kedah here today.
Kota Setar police chief ACP Syed Basri Syed Ali said the body of Saw Chee Wan, 16, was identified by his family members, who had earlier reported him missing from his home in Kampung Seberang Perak near here, located about two metres from the scene.
"The body was found lodged against a tree near Taman Pesisiran Jeragan, Ampang Jajar, by a cleaning worker from the Alor Setar City Council (MBAS) at 9am today," he told a press conference at the scene.
He said the body of another man, a convert to Islam identified as Muhammad Amin, or N Thiagarah, 46, from Tanjung Bendahara here, had also been reported missing on the same day, June 12.
"Investigations found that the man was a psychiatric patient. The bodies of both men were found about 100 metres apart, but investigations revealed that they were not related to each other in any way.
"A team from the Forensic Unit of the Kedah Police Contingent Headquarters arrived at the scene and conducted examinations, finding no elements of foul play," said Syed Basri.
He said the bodies were sent to the Forensic Department of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) for further action.
"I urge the public not to make any speculation regarding the discovery of the two male bodies," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 5 arrested over robberies and assaults via fake dating profiles in Johor
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Police have arrested five men linked to a robbery syndicate targeting victims met through a gay dating application.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the suspects, aged 21 to 28, were detained at 11.30am in Brinchang, Cameron Highlands, Pahang, by a team from the Johor Baru Selatan district police headquarters (IPD).
He said nine local men, aged 25 to 40, had fallen victim to the group, with losses totalling RM315,220.
The robberies took place in Taman Pelangi and Kampung Majidee between January and May this year.
"The suspects used the application to identify victims and arranged to meet them in a car.
"Accomplices would then pose as security personnel and intimidate victims by accusing them of engaging in indecent activities inside vehicles in public areas," he said at a press conference at the Iskandar Puteri IPD today.
He said the victims were then assaulted and their belongings, including debit and credit cards, jewellery and cash, were taken.
The suspects also obtained victims' bank card PIN numbers, withdrew money from their accounts and used it to purchase gold jewellery.
"Investigations found that statements obtained from the suspects were consistent with the robberies reported by the victims," he said.
Ab Rahaman said police also seized five mobile phones, a Perodua Bezza, a gold purchase receipt, five debit cards, four necklaces, four pairs of earrings, two rings and two gold bracelets.
He added that checks showed all five suspects had prior criminal records involving robbery and drugs, but they tested negative for drugs.
The case is being investigated under Section 394 of the Penal Code, and three suspects will be charged at the Johor Baru Sessions Court tomorrow.
Under Section 394, anyone who injures a victim during the commission of a robbery can be punished with imprisonment of up to 20 years, and is also liable to caning. -- Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MQA: Malaysian higher education meets international standards
Article:
SINTOK: The quality of Malaysia's higher education remains internationally recognised and should not be linked to concerns over graduate employability.
Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA) chief executive officer Professor Datuk Dr Mohammad Shatar Sabran said graduates today need to equip themselves with added value and a broader range of skills.
This, he said, was to remain competitive in an increasingly challenging job market, as strong academic results alone are no longer sufficient to secure employment.
Shatar said competition for jobs is intense, requiring graduates to develop additional competencies such as multilingual proficiency, critical thinking and the ability to adapt to technological advancements and evolving industry demands.
"A good cumulative grade point average (CGPA) may help a graduate secure an interview, but getting the job depends on the added value that the individual brings.
"Academic achievement is a basic requirement, but today's workplace demands much more than that," he said at the "Graduate Employability: Between Reality and Expectations" programme at Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM) here today.
Shatar also dismissed claims that declining standards in Malaysia's higher education system were responsible for graduates facing difficulties in securing employment.
He said all programmes offered by public and private higher education institutions accredited by MQA meet international standards and are developed with industry requirements in mind.
Meanwhile, UUM vice-chancellor Professor Dr Ahmad Martadha Mohamed said the university continues to strengthen its curriculum to ensure programmes remain aligned with current industry needs, including by incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) elements across various fields of study.
He said UUM regularly seeks feedback from industry players through its industry advisory council and is planning to establish an international industry advisory panel comprising representatives from multinational companies to ensure its programmes remain relevant.
Martadha added that UUM also places emphasis on professional certifications and runs the School of Hard Knocks (SoHK) programme, which provides final-year students with industry exposure to enhance their employability upon graduation.
"The programmes we offer are continuously improved to ensure UUM graduates remain competitive and are sought after by employers both nationally and internationally," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HLIB turns bullish on Oriental Kopi, lifts call to 'Buy'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Bank Bhd (HLIB) said shares of Oriental Kopi Holdings Bhd may offer a more attractive risk-reward profile following a sharp correction, with the bank seeing upside potential as the stock stabilises near key support levels.
The stock, which is trading around the 92.5 sen level, has fallen about 42.5 per cent from its peak of RM1.61 after a double-top breakdown and is currently trading below key moving averages, signalling a bearish near-term trend.
"That said, price action is deeply oversold and stabilising around 89 sen, which aligns with the DT measured target. This suggests the stock is approaching a potential exhaustion zone, offering a more favourable risk-reward setup," the bank said in a note.
Following the recent share price weakness, HLIB upgraded the stock to "Buy" from "Hold", with a target price of RM1.28, citing favourable valuations, scalable growth potential and its positioning within Malaysia's listed food and beverage sector.
HLIB said a sustained move above RM1.00 could trigger upside towards RM1.06 and RM1.20, while downside risks remain towards 86 sen and 82 sen should the stock breach support.
A cut-loss level is placed at 85.5 sen, with an alternative trading setup pointing to a RM1.25 upside target and 94 sen cut loss.
"Despite near-term headwinds from margin pressure, expansion costs and moderating earnings momentum, Oriental Kopi's medium-term prospects remain compelling. This is underpinned by its limited exposure to volatile fuel and raw material costs, strong domestic brand equity, and a sustained outlet expansion strategy," HLIB said.
The bank said the company's growth prospects are also expected to benefit from early traction in overseas markets through fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) distribution partnerships and new outlet rollouts planned in 2026.
It added that the Visit Malaysia 2026 campaign could provide an additional earnings catalyst, supported by Oriental Kopi's presence in tourist-heavy locations such as malls and airports, where foreign visitors historically contribute between 5.0 per cent and 10 per cent of the company's group revenue.
Oriental Kopi posted a 19.2 per cent rise in net profit to RM32.1 million for the first half ended March 31, 2026 (1HFY26), driven by strong café expansion and higher sales from packaged food products.
Its revenue for the six-month period surged 42.5 per cent to a record RM286.5 million from RM201 million a year earlier.
The café chain operations remained the company's main earnings contributor, accounting for RM257 million or 89.7 per cent of total revenue, while distribution and retail of packaged food products contributed RM26.8 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Authorities arrest 353 people in forest reserve encroachment crackdown
Article:
KOTA BARU: Authorities have arrested 353 people, including Indonesians, Myanmar nationals, Bangladeshis and Cambodians, for offences involving forest reserve encroachment since last year, with Kelantan recording the most cases.
Peninsular Malaysia Forestry Department director-general Datuk Zahari Ibrahim said 202 people were detained in 2025, while another 151 were arrested between January and March this year.
Of those arrested last year, 177 were Malaysians and 25 were foreign nationals from Indonesia, Myanmar, Bangladesh and Cambodia, he said.
"For the first three months of this year, 125 Malaysians and 26 foreign nationals from eight countries were detained for offences under the National Forestry Act 1984," he added.
Zahari said the department recorded 162 cases of permanent forest reserve encroachment nationwide in 2025 and another 45 cases between January and March this year.
Kelantan recorded the highest number of cases, with 24 incidents last year and nine more in the first quarter of 2026, he said.
"The statistics show that encroachers remain undeterred and continue seeking opportunities to penetrate permanent forest reserve areas," he said.
He said all offenders were found to have committed offences under the National Forestry Act 1984, with most cases involving entry into closed forest areas without valid permits under Section 47, illegal occupation or land clearing for agriculture and construction under Section 32, and the unlawful harvesting, felling and theft of timber under Sections 81 and 15.
Zahari said enforcement operations also led to the seizure and protection of forest resources worth RM3.55 million.
"The value of seizures amounted to RM3.1 million in 2025 and RM450,000 between January and March this year," he said.
He said Peninsular Malaysia has 5.73 million ha of forested land, representing 43.2 per cent of its total land area.
Of the total forested area, 4.85 million ha, or 84.8 per cent, have been gazetted as permanent reserved forests under the National Forestry Act, he said.
Zahari said that permanent reserved forests are national assets protected by law and warned that enforcement action would continue without compromise.
"Encroachment methods are becoming increasingly sophisticated. However, with the use of surveillance technologies such as drones and satellite monitoring, as well as close cooperation with security agencies, we will continue to take firm action against offenders under the National Forestry Act," he said.
He added that the department would continue strengthening enforcement and cooperation with other agencies to curb illegal activities that threaten the country's forest resources and biodiversity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HLIB keeps 'Buy' on IJM, sees upside after 13.7pct share price rebound
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Investment Bank (HLIB) has kept its "Buy" call on IJM Corp Bhd, with a target price of RM3.02 that is based on a 20 per cent discount to its sum-of-parts (SOP) valuation of RM3.77.
IJM's share price has rebounded 13.7 per cent to RM2.33 as of June 5, after falling 31 per cent from its year-to-date high of RM2.97 on Jan 13 to a low of RM2.05 on May 20.
HLIB attributed the earlier decline to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and the collapse of Sunway's RM11 billion conditional voluntary takeover offer at RM3.15 per share.
It said the stock's recent price movement suggests the correction phase may be easing, with technical indicators pointing to a potential accumulation trend.
The bank said IJM's recent share price correction had brought valuations to more attractive levels, with the stock trading at 16.6 times and 16.8 times projected price-to-earnings ratios for financial years 2027 and 2028 (FY27/FY28), respectively.
"The risk-reward profile is highly favourable with operational nadirs in the rearview mirror. Successful execution of its value-unlocking initiatives remains the key re-rating catalyst," HLIB said in a note while advising investors to cut losses at RM2.15 should the stock weaken further.
HLIB expects operational momentum to strengthen across IJM's key divisions, supported by a solid construction order book and improving property sales.
The construction division remains backed by RM7.1 billion in unbilled orders and an RM18 billion tender pipeline, with management targeting RM9 billion in new wins in FY27, driven partly by accelerating data centre-related projects.
In the property segment, billings are expected to have bottomed in FY26, while stronger sales have lifted unbilled sales to RM2 billion, the bank said.
The industry division continues to benefit from firm demand, supported by a 1.0 million-tonne spun pile order book, while port operations are expected to recover following the completion of major maintenance works by a key customer.
IJM also declared a second interim dividend of 5.0 sen per share and a special dividend of 1 sen.
"More notably, management introduced a RM3 billion capital distribution target over the next three years, equivalent to a cumulative 83 sen dividend per share (or about 28 sen per annum), implying an attractive 12 per cent average yield," HLIB said.
HLIB said the capital return plan is supported by value-unlocking initiatives, including a potential listing of IJM's construction arm, monetisation of toll assets and a planned exit from its Indian operations within two years, alongside a formalised 40 per cent core profit attributable to shareholders payout policy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CIMB maintains 'Buy' on QL Resources, sees re-rating potential on earnings recovery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd has maintained a "Buy" call on QL Resources Bhd with a target price of RM4.60.
"We believe value has emerged post the 24.2 per cent share price retracement over the past 12 months, as QL Resources returns to an earnings growth trajectory.
"We see scope for QL Resources to re-rate closer to its five-year mean, backed by its diversified earnings base, defensive demand profile, and strong execution track record," it added. Ends
CIMB Securities said that the company's outlook remains positive backed by its multi-year growth strategies across all four business segments and the relatively defensive demand profile of its core products.
It added that QL Resources has earmarked an RM2.6 billion capital expenditure (capex) programme for the financial year 2027 (FY27) to FY32.
The firm said the capex allocation will be led by its integrated livestock farming (ILF) segment, which accounts for 42 per cent of the planned spending, and marine products manufacturing (MPM) at 32 per cent.
"This will be followed by a convenience store chain at 19 per cent and palm oil and clean energy at seven per cent. This should underpin capacity expansion, downstream value-add, and longer-term earnings resilience," it said.
Meanwhile, CIMB Securities said QL Resources is intensifying efforts to build a more recurring income base via services and maintenance, alongside asset development in solar and battery energy storage systems.
Post the FY26 briefing, the firm has reiterated its FY27 to FY29 earnings forecasts on the stock.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain in almost entire country up to 6pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in almost all states apart from Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya until 6pm today.
MetMalaysia in its warning at 2.25pm said the weather conditions are expected to occur throughout the states of Perak, Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
In Kedah, the areas affected are Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu, while in Penang it includes Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan.
The weather conditions are also predicted to occur in Terengganu involving Dungun and Kemaman, while in Pahang it involves Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, Maran, Kuantan, Pekan and Rompin.
In Negri Sembilan, it involves the areas of Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah and Rembau, while in Johor it includes Kluang, Mersing and Kota Tinggi.
For Sarawak, the areas predicted to be affected are Sarikei (Julau), Sibu (Sibu and Selangau), Mukah (Matu), Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Telang Usan) and Limbang (Lawas).
Meanwhile, in Sabah, warnings have been issued for the Interior (Sipitang, Tenom, Beaufort, Keningau and Tambunan), West Coast, Tawau (Tawau, Kunak and Lahad Datu) and Sandakan  (Telupid, Kinabatangan and Sandakan). - BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PTPTN sets aside RM3bil to help students pursue higher education
Article:
TASEK GELUGOR: The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated about RM3 billion this year to support students pursuing higher education at institutions of higher learning nationwide.
Its chairman Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim said the allocation, channelled through PTPTN education loans, is aimed at ensuring no student is deprived of tertiary education due to financial constraints.
"Students offered places at universities, polytechnics and related institutions can apply for financing through the MyPTPTN application. Each year, we allocate about RM3 billion to assist both new and existing students.
"We do not want any student to miss the opportunity to further their studies because of financial difficulties," she said when met at the Penang Regional Development Authority (Perda) Santuni Komuniti Madani programme at Masjid Jamek Al-Islah in Pokok Tampang here today.
Norliza said PTPTN also provides an advance loan payment (WPP) of RM1,500 to help new students meet initial expenses before registering at their institutions.
She said the assistance could be used for registration fees, learning materials and other early study-related expenses.
"Students in need of the WPP can apply through MyPTPTN," she said.
Norliza added that PTPTN had also introduced the PTPTN eKasih Children's Scholarship, which was launched in Johor yesterday, to assist students from low-income families.
The scholarship is expected to benefit 10,000 students nationwide in 2026, particularly those from households earning below RM4,000 a month.
"Should there be a need to assist more students, we will consider additional applications based on the stipulated eligibility criteria," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MetMalaysia steps up preparations for El Nino-influenced hot, dry weather
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) is intensifying the monitoring of the El Nino phenomenon that is expected to begin influencing weather patterns soon, till early next year.
Its director-general, Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip, said the phenomenon is expected to result in hotter and drier-than-usual conditions, particularly during the southwest monsoon period, which started on May 14 and will continue until September.
"The reduced rainfall in certain areas could increase the risk of water shortages, forest and peatland fires, as well as haze incidents," he said in a statement today.
Hisham said the department has stepped up preparedness measures by strengthening the dissemination of weather information and early warnings to government agencies, stakeholders and the public.
"As of this month, the department has carried out more than 70 briefings, meetings and engagement programmes at national, state and district levels focusing on hot and dry weather conditions."
These included briefings to the Economy Ministry, the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry, the National Economic Action Council and the Agriculture Department, as well as presentations at state and district disaster management committee meetings nationwide.
"MetMalaysia also provides up to seven-day forecasts on forest and peatland fire risks, issues haze forecasts and stands ready to support cloud seeding operations when required," said Hisham.
He advised the public to stay updated via the myCuaca mobile application and the department's official website, practise water conservation and avoid open burning activities.
kw: Malaysia, news, nation, El Nino, MetMalaysia, southwest monsoon, hot weather, dry weather, myCuaca, monitoring, NST, Malaysia news
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CIMB raises MPI target price to RM57, maintains 'Buy' on stronger AI demand outlook
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd has reiterated its "Buy" recommendation on Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI) and raised its target price to RM57 from RM50, citing improved visibility in artificial intelligence (AI)-driven demand and firmer investor sentiment toward the technology sector.
"We view the recent tech sector pullback as an attractive opportunity to accumulate the stock," it said in a note.
CIMB Securities said MPI is expected to benefit from its recent development of new packaging solutions for voltage power delivery (VPD)-enabled power modules targeting artificial intelligence (AI) data centre applications.
According to the research firm, MPI is currently developing the solutions at its Carsem Suzhou facility, with mass production expected to commence in the financial year 2027 (FY27).
The firm believes this will provide an additional growth avenue for its AI-related revenue in FY27 to FY28.
"We also expect growing adoption of 800 VDC server rack architectures to drive higher demand for wide bandgap power solutions, creating further content value opportunities for MPI.
"We expect AI-related revenue to contribute about 20 to 25 per cent of its FY27 to FY28F revenue," it said in a note.
CIMB Securities has raised MPI's FY28 earning per share by 9.1 per cent to reflect stronger growth in next-generation power modules and inventory replenishment in the automotive sector.
The firm now forecast MPI to deliver a robust FY25 to FY28 core net profit of 32 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RHB keeps 'Overweight' on banking sector, cites attractive yields despite modest earnings growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's banking sector is expected to deliver a stronger performance in the second quarter (Q2) of 2026 supported by stabilising marked-to-market (MTM) losses, improving investor sentiment and wealth traction.
RHB Research said the sector had a subdued start to the year as weaker non-II plus a rise in credit cost, partly due to a base effect, dampened bottom-line growth.
It noted that for now, the majority of banks retained their 2026 guidance and targets, with expectations that the Middle East situation and elevated energy prices would not persist, coupled with improved earnings momentum post-quarter end.
"Malaysian banks think the first-order impact from the Middle East conflict is limited, as direct exposures are not significant.
"However, banks continue to monitor potential second- and third-order effects and watch over sectors such as transport and logistics, agriculture, construction and infrastructure, as well as segments such as small and medium enterprises (SMEs)," it said.
RHB Research believes that the banking sector in Q2 2026 appears to be a better quarter sequentially as MTM losses suffered in Q1 2026 stabilise while some banks saw improved investor sentiment and wealth traction.
Also, it noted that some banks see opportunities to reprice deposits lower in Q2, to help support net interest margin (NIM).
"Some banks think there could be opportunities to further optimise their asset and liability mix to aid NIM expansion," it said.
On the flip side, a more cautious approach focused on credit quality could temper asset yields, it added.
Assuming the base case scenario that the elevated energy prices do not persist, RHB Research said banks think existing overlays and provision buffers should be sufficient to help cushion the potential emerging risks ahead.
Overall, the firm said while sector earnings growth post-adjustments is not too exciting, valuation is decent and dividend yield looks attractive.
RHB Research maintained its "Overweight" stance on the banking sector.
"Our top picks include Public Bank Bhd, Malayan Banking Bhd and AMMB Holdings Bhd," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PublicInvest cautious on SDS outlook despite FY27 earnings boost from Mamee plants
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) expects the factories acquired by SDS Group Bhd from Mamee Bakery Sdn Bhd to start contributing progressively to earnings in the financial year ending March 31, 2027 (FY27).
The research house said Plant 1 is already operational after undergoing renovation works and securing the necessary regulatory approvals, while Plant 2 is currently being refurbished, and Plant 3 is expected to be upgraded sequentially.
It maintained its earnings forecasts for the Johor Bahru-based manufacturer and retailer of bakery products.
SDS acquired Mamee Bakery's assets in Plentong for RM28 million, a move aimed at expanding its manufacturing capacity and supporting longer-term growth. Its indirect wholly-owned subsidiary, SDS Top Baker Sdn Bhd, signed the agreements in August 2025.
The newly acquired facilities are expected to strengthen production capabilities, particularly for London Roll products, which are seen as a new earnings stream for the company as it diversifies its food manufacturing portfolio.
However, Publicinvest has adopted a cautious stance on SDS Group's near-term outlook, citing elevated oil prices and softer consumer spending.
"While London Roll products present a new earnings stream over the longer term, near- term visibility remains limited as customer base buildout will take time," it said.
It noted that packaging material costs remain particularly exposed to potential price increases due to their petroleum-based inputs, adding pressure on margins.
"Softer consumer spending poses a risk to the higher-margin retail segment. The wholesale segment provides a degree of earnings stability, though its margins remain vulnerable to diesel subsidy rationalisation, with national supply secured only until July 2026," it said.
On a more positive note, PublicInvest said the company targets to open three new retail outlets in the financial year 2027 (FY27), and is exploring wholesale route expansion to broaden its earnings base.
Overall, PublicInvest maintained its "Neutral" call on SDS Group with a revised lower target price of 45 sen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Transport firms' diesel subsidies now assessed based on usage pattern - Fuziah
Article:
KUANTAN: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry is reviewing the diesel subsidy mechanism for land goods transport companies, focusing on usage-based allocations rather than a blanket increase.
Its deputy minister, Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh, said the ministry is reviewing available data to ensure subsidies are distributed according to actual needs and operational performance.
"Under the proposed approach, each company will be assessed based on factors such as the number of lorries it operates, total distance travelled and fuel consumption history.
"This data-driven method aims to determine the appropriate level of subsidy required for each entity, " she told reporters after attending the Rahmah Madani Sales programme held in conjunction with the opening of a new chain supermarket branch here today.
Fuziah said some stakeholders have raised concerns and proposed increases in subsidy allocations, but the ministry maintains that any adjustments will be guided by usage patterns and operational efficiency.
Earlier, Fuziah was asked about the ministry's response to growing requests from transport companies for higher diesel subsidies.
Starting June 1, the government
revised the fixed quota
limits under the Subsidised Diesel Control System for the land goods transport sector to curb subsidy leakages, with each fleet card allocated a quota ranging from 900 litres to 5,000 litres per month.
Companies requiring fuel beyond the stipulated limit may submit appeals for quota increases to the Petroleum Subsidy Approval Committee, which is managed by the ministry.
Meanwhile, Fuziah said she would raise a proposal to increase the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) initiative fuel subsidy allocation for registered media practitioners with the Finance Ministry.
"I will bring this matter to the Finance Ministry and highlight the concerns faced by media practitioners, especially given the extensive travel required in their work," she said.
In April, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the proposal to
retain the 300-litre monthly Budi95 fuel quota
for media practitioners, raised by the Gabungan Kelab Media Malaysia, will be tabled at the National Economic Action Council meeting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cases involving Najib, Akmal Saleh among several court highlights this coming week
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Several high-profile criminal and constitutional cases are scheduled to come before the courts this week, including proceedings involving former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak.
Others include former armed forces chief General (Rtd) Tan Sri Mohd Nizam Jaffar, Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh and Muhammed Yusoff Rawther.
The Court of Appeal in Putrajaya on Monday is set to hear the prosecution's appeals against the High Court's decision to acquit and discharge Yusoff at the close of the prosecution's case on charges of drug trafficking and possession of firearms.
The appeals challenge the High Court's ruling in relation to charges under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and Section 36(1) of the Firearms Act 1960. The case attracted widespread public interest due to Yusoff's former association with Anwar.
Also on Monday, the Sessions Court will hear the mention of the case involving three men accused of assaulting a 47-year-old man at a restaurant in Kepong last month, videos of which went viral.
Wong Weng Luen, Lai Kae Bin and Wan Ken Fei are charged with causing grievous hurt using dangerous weapons during the May 7 incident.
On Tuesday, the High Court in Penang will hear Dr Akmal's application for judicial review against the attorney-general's decision to prosecute him under Section 505(b) of the Penal Code for allegedly making a statement conducive to public mischief.
The application is expected to touch on constitutional and public law issues, including claims of selective prosecution, equality before the law under Article 8 of the Federal Constitution and the scope of prosecutorial discretion.
Attention will return to Putrajaya on Thursday when the Court of Appeal conducts case management in Najib's application to adduce fresh evidence in his appeal against his conviction in the RM2.27 billion 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) case.
The former prime minister is seeking to introduce evidence said to relate to fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, and former 1MDB general counsel Jasmine Loo Ai Swan.
The Sessions Court here is also scheduled to conduct further case management in the corruption case involving Nizam.
The retired general faces four charges linked to alleged abuse of power involving contracts worth more than RM3.5 million, criminal breach of trust involving RM3 million from the Armed Forces Welfare Fund and accepting RM200,000 from a company director.
The defence is expected to receive the final tranche of prosecution documents before deciding on its next course of action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lost but unharmed: Four women found safe after Bukit Tunggul hike
Article:
SEPANG: Four women who became lost while hiking at Bukit Tunggul were found safe and brought out of the area by firefighters today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the
hikers had lost their way
during the climb.
"A search operation was launched after contact was established with the victims, who were aged between 25 and 40.
"All four women were successfully located by firefighters and escorted to a safe area. None of them sustained any injuries," he said when contacted.
Mukhlis said personnel from the Dengkil Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene at 10.50am and immediately began efforts to trace the hikers.
The operation, involving six firefighters led by operations commander Muhamad Sakirin Selamat, was completed at 11.30am.
The hikers were assessed at the scene and did not require further medical treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Produce evidence of alleged Negri Sembilan plot - Asyraf
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has rejected allegations that the party was involved in efforts to destabilise or bring down the Negri Sembilan state government, said its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
Asyraf said that if there were allegations that Umno had been involved in any undemocratic effort to bring down the state government before the end of its term, such allegations must be accompanied by clear evidence and facts.
"Do not throw general accusations that create the perception that Umno has acted disloyally," he told the New Straits Times today.
Asyraf said Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) had provided support to the state government until the expiry of its mandate, and had remained committed to ensuring administrative stability throughout that period.
"Umno has never betrayed the commitments that were agreed upon," he said.
He said once the state assembly's mandate had ended and elections were called, every party had the democratic right to determine its own position, strategy and offer a fresh mandate to the people.
"Competition in elections cannot be equated with betrayal or efforts to topple a government," he said.
He highlighted that Umno continued to respect the spirit of the unity government at the federal level, but said political cooperation did not mean any party had to sacrifice its identity, principles and right to seek a mandate from the people in an election.
Earlier today, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim maintained that efforts to destabilise the Negri Sembilan government amounted to a form of betrayal, even as Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi
rejected suggestions
that Umno had acted disloyally.
Anwar said his position was based on what he saw as deliberate attempts to undermine a government that had been functioning in conditions of political and economic stability.
Yesterday, Zahid, who is also Umno president,
rejected claims
that the party had betrayed
its allies in Negri Sembilan, saying there was no question of disloyalty once a government's term had ended.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khind expands rent-to-own push with first physical store launch
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Khind Malaysia has launched its first physical rent-to-own (RTO) store in Semenyih, expanding its consumer financing model beyond digital platforms as it continues to evolve from its roots as a small electrical repair shop.
Founded in 1961 in Sekinchan, Selangor, Khind began as a modest electrical repair business before gradually expanding through product innovation, distribution growth and brand development into a home solutions company.
The company, operated by the Cheng family and listed on Bursa Malaysia as Khind Holdings Bhd, said the milestone reflects decades of transformation driven by continuous innovation in household appliances and customer-focused solutions.
The new outlet marks a key step in Khind's RTO strategy, which allows consumers to access essential home appliances such as washer-dryers, refrigerators and air-conditioning units through flexible monthly payments instead of upfront purchases, aimed at improving affordability for households managing rising living costs.
"We chose Semenyih because it represents the growth of new Malaysian communities, including young families, first-time homeowners, and rapidly growing townships," said Khind Group executive chairman Cheng Ping Keat.
"Our role is to support them by removing the burden of high upfront costs and making essential appliances more accessible and manageable through a monthly plan," he added.
The expansion strengthens the company's RTO presence beyond digital channels, bringing its home appliance ownership solutions closer to local communities while supporting future growth beyond the Klang Valley.
The Semenyih store is designed as a real home-like environment where customers can explore appliances in practical living setups while receiving direct consultation from RTO advisors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid welcomes new political party, urges unity
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has welcomed the formation of a new Malay-based political party, expressing hope that it would strengthen unity rather than divide the community.
He said any individual or political figure was free to establish a new political platform, provided it contributed towards uniting Malays and advancing national interests.
"We welcome any individual or leader, as long as we can unite our thinking and avoid further divisions among ourselves.
"Whatever platform is used, if it ultimately helps unite our actions in support of the national agenda, the Islamic agenda and the interests of our community, then, God willing, we can lend our support.
"We have also come together previously through discourse and discussions under the National Collaboration initiative," he said after attending the Zikir Peringatan Haul Asmaulhusna 2026 programme at Sultan Iskandar Mosque in Bandar Dato' Onn here today.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, said differing views should be channelled towards strengthening the national agenda, Islam and the interests of Malays, while continuing to respect the rights of other races and religions in the country.
He added that Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative had successfully contributed to fostering national unity.
Yesterday,
Gerakan Reset Malaysia chairman Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudi
n had announced that the movement would operate under a newly established political party known as Parti Wawasan Negara as part of efforts to strengthen political cooperation with various blocs.
Hamzah had said the party would serve as a platform for unity and work alongside PAS and other like-minded parties to build a broader and more convincing political understanding.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BTS ticket scam: Police track suspected mule bank accounts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in scams involving ticket sales for South Korean K-pop supergroup BTS's concert in December.
Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing, with several bank accounts identified so far.
"We suspect these accounts are being used by syndicates to receive payments from victims.
"It is possible that these bank accounts are mule accounts," he said when contacted today.
The BTS World Tour 'Arirang' in Kuala Lumpur is scheduled for Dec 12-13.
The seven-member South Korean group have previously sold out major venues like London's Wembley Stadium and the Rose Bowl in Pasadena, California.
Previously, Hoo said all 28 police reports lodged were under investigation and urged fans to buy tickets only through official channels provided by concert organisers.
Police believe victims were lured by social media advertisements and offers from unauthorised agents claiming to have access to discounted tickets or seats that had already sold out.
The warning comes amid overwhelming demand for BTS concert tickets, which sold out shortly after sales opened, creating opportunities for scammers to target fans seeking alternative sources.
In one reported case, a local fan allegedly lost RM3,000 after dealing with a ticket reseller on social media. The victim made several payments before the seller disappeared, deleted chat records and changed usernames.
Police advised the public to verify ticket sellers, avoid payments to unknown individuals and contact the National Scam Response Centre (NSRC) on 997 if they suspect fraud.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rosmah lodges police report over US$13mil luxury mansion purchase claims [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has lodged a police report over social media allegations linking her to the purported purchase of a US$13 million luxury property for her son-in-law.
The posts, said to have been circulated on X and Facebook, claimed the wife of jailed former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak was involved in financing the acquisition of the property.
Rosmah denied all the allegations, describing them as baseless and unsupported by credible evidence.
"The allegations purporting to implicate me in the use of shell companies, offshore financial structures, or associations with any of the individuals or entities named in these videos are wholly without foundation, utterly false, and maliciously fabricated.
"They are unsupported by a single shred of credible evidence and represent nothing more than a calculated and deliberate campaign to defame me and to bring dishonour upon my family," she said in a statement.
Rosmah said she recalls no involvement in the purchase, financing, transfer or ownership of any such property, and maintained that the allegations were intended to damage her reputation and that of her family.
She said a police report has been lodged and she anticipates further investigations from authorities into the individuals or parties related to the social media post and that she will give full cooperation to ensure a complete and thorough investigation.
She also called for social media platforms to remove, disable and prevent further circulation of the posts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM10mil allocated for programme to support 1,300 retiring armed forces personnel
Article:
SEGAMAT: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) has allocated RM10 million this year for the implementation of its Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme, benefiting about 1,300 Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) personnel approaching retirement.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the allocation for the intervention programme forms part of the ministry's efforts to safeguard the welfare and livelihood of military veterans.
He said the WBL programme enables the experience and expertise acquired by personnel throughout their service in the MAF to be formally assessed and recognised in collaboration with higher education institutions.
Under the programme, personnel can be awarded diplomas aligned with their areas of expertise, including culinary arts, hospitality, sports coaching and management, without having to sit for formal examinations.
"Most personnel join the military with only the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) qualification. When they retire at the age of 41 or 42, they enter the job market with the same qualifications, making it difficult for them to compete for employment opportunities.
"Through the WBL programme, they leave service with at least a diploma, with the RM6,500 assessment cost for each individual fully borne by the government," he told a press conference after a working visit to the 4th Royal Artillery Regiment at Segamat Camp here today.
Meanwhile, Mohamed Khaled said the ministry has also revamped the transition training programme conducted by the Armed Forces Veterans Affairs Corporation (Perhebat) by aligning it more closely with industry requirements.
He said the move is intended to ensure that personnel completing the six-month transition training programme can be directly absorbed into suitable employment sectors without having to search for jobs on their own, while earning a dignified income.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zaid Ibrahim vows to reshape Pas image, says party can lead fairer Malaysia
Article:
KOTA BARU: Former de facto law minister Datuk Zaid Ibrahim has pledged to help reshape public perception of Pas, describing the party as capable of governing a multicultural Malaysia based on justice and equality.
Zaid, who officially joined Pas together with 13 other political figures ahead of the Negri Sembilan state election, said he was warmly received by the party's top leadership when he was admitted as a member last night.
"They're warm, sincere and friendly. I will repay their faith. I will work hard to dispel the image of Pas as an extreme anti-democratic party, not suitable for a multicultural Malaysia," he said.
The former Kota Baru member of parliament said Pas was, in his view, the only Malay-majority political party with the determination to address inequality, hegemony and preferential treatment based on class.
"The essence of Islam will be the governing principle," he said.
Zaid added that a Pas-led administration would uphold fairness and reject double standards.
"You will not have, under Pas rule, (a situation) where we are described as equal, but some are more equal than others," he said.
He also expressed confidence that Pas would play a leading role in forming the next federal government.
"Pas will govern Malaysia together with like-minded progressive MPs after the next general election," he said.
Zaid's entry into Pas comes amid a wider political realignment within the opposition bloc following the party's recent decision to end its political cooperation with Bersatu and pursue broader alliances ahead of upcoming state polls and the next general election.
His membership was announced alongside
13 other politicians
during a Pas event held in conjunction with preparations for the Negri Sembilan election in Seremban.
Among the others whose memberships were announced were former Negri Sembilan state executive councillor and Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Dr Mohamad Rafie Abdul Malek and former private secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Rahimi Osman.
Also joining Pas are former Perak state executive councillor and former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman, and former Negeri Sembilan Amanah chairman Dr Rosli Yaakup, who is also a former Pas commissioner for the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Onn Hafiz has attended Transport Ministry functions many times before
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has hit back at Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's claims about not being invited to Transport Ministry events.
The transport minister said he and his party harboured no ill will towards Onn Hafiz despite recent exchanges between the two.
He said DAP would not force anyone to work with the party.
"If he does not want to sit with me, that's okay. DAP does not regard anyone as an enemy. DAP is a pragmatic party and not an arrogant party. We do not force anyone," he said in a video posted on his Facebook page.
The remarks came amid a public exchange after Onn Hafiz said he would rather not hold the menteri besar's post than sit at the same table with DAP in the Johor government.
In a separate Facebook post, Loke also responded to Onn Hafiz's claim that the Johor administration had not been invited to several official Transport Ministry events over the past three years.
Thanking Onn Hafiz for accepting his invitation to ride on the KTM Komuter Selatan service, he said he wished to "refresh" the Johor Umno chief's memory regarding a number of Transport Ministry programmes attended either by the menteri besar himself or representatives of the Johor government.
Loke said Onn Hafiz had attended several major transport-related events, including the Jan 11, 2024 RTS Link ceremony witnessed by the prime ministers of Malaysia and Singapore; the June 30, 2025 event marking the arrival of the first RTS Link train in Singapore; the Aug 23, 2025 launch of the ETS3 service and opening of the KTM Kluang station by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong; and, the Dec 11, 2025 ceremony marking the completion of the Gemas-Johor Baru double-tracking project and the launch of ETS services to Johor Bahru.
He also noted that invitations had been extended for other ministry programmes, including the MyLesen B2 programme for school students in Johor Baru on May 11 this year, but no representative from the state government was present.
Loke said representatives of the Johor government had also attended several Transport Ministry-related events, including the signing of a memorandum of understanding for the Kluang Heritage Railway Linear Park project on Sept 7, 2023, a working visit to the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex Customs, Immigration and Quarantine complex on Jan 11, 2024, and the reopening of the Pasir Gudang-Kempas Baru railway line on May 9, 2025.
"I understand election season can be busy. Perhaps some things were forgotten," he said.
Loke also drew a distinction between official functions and working visits, saying he did not wish to burden the menteri besar with invitations to every working visit undertaken by the ministry.
Nevertheless, he said an official invitation to the launch of the KTM Komuter Selatan service would be delivered promptly and that Onn Hafiz would be invited to deliver a speech at the event in Kulai on Tuesday.
Loke also noted that Johor's executive councillor in charge of the transport portfolio had participated in a trial run of the Komuter Selatan service on March 3 this year, before the service details had been finalised, adding that the ministry had not been informed of the visit.
On Friday, Loke invited Onn Hafiz to join him on a train ride to mark the launch of the KTM Komuter Selatan service from Kulai to Johor Baru.
In response, Onn Hafiz said he was willing to attend and appreciated the invitation, but claimed the state government was invited late, despite the event having been planned more than a week earlier.
The Johor Barisan Nasional chairman also listed several ministry events between 2023 and 2025 which, he claimed, neither he nor the relevant state executive councillor had been invited to attend.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ETS back on track after power outage, but eight services running late
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Repair works following the overhead line equipment (ATA) outage at KM 458.872 between Tiroi and Seremban Stations have been completed and electric train (ETS) services are now back in operation.
However, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) in a statement today said that several ETS services on the KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral route are still experiencing temporary delays to enable the operation schedule to be stabilised.
KTMB said as of 3.30 pm today, eight ETS services are still experiencing delays, namely train EX9573 which is three hours and 34 minutes late, EP9524 (two hours and 35 minutes), EP9326 (one hour and 40 minutes), EP9428 (25 minutes).
Also experiencing delays are trains EP9323 (two hours and 11 minutes), EG9343 (one hour and 14 minutes), EG9442 (two hours and 51 minutes) and EX9575 (one hour).
According to KTMB, ETS passengers who are affected by train delays of more than two hours and choose not to continue their journey can get a full fare refund at the ticket counter.
“As a caring measure and to reduce the inconvenience experienced by passengers, KTMB is also providing food and light refreshments to affected passengers for train services that are delayed by more than two hours as a token of appreciation for their patience and cooperation throughout the disruption,” the statement said.
In addition, KTMB auxiliary police personnel together with operations staff at Seremban Station also helped manage passenger movements and channel the latest information to ensure the smooth running of affected passengers.
According to KTMB, as soon as the disruption was identified, an initial notification was sent via the Short Message System (SMS) to affected passengers to inform them of the current status and expected delays to enable them to plan their journeys better.
“KTMB is currently closely monitoring train movements to ensure that the operational schedule is fully restored as soon as possible,” the statement said. — Bernama

Title: Maybe just stay home tonight: Storms sweeping Malaysia until 9pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning forecasting thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds across almost all states and Kuala Lumpur until 9pm today.
MetMalaysia, in a warning issued at 4.35pm, said adverse weather conditions are expected in Perlis, Penang, Perak and Kuala Lumpur.
Other states expected to be affected are Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu), Kelantan (Gua Musang), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin).
In Selangor, the areas likely to be affected are Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat; in Negeri Sembilan, Jelebu, Jempol and Tampin; in Melaka, Jasin; and in Johor, Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Pontian, Kulai and Johor Bahru.
In Sarawak, similar weather conditions are expected in Sebauh (Bintulu), Beluru, Telang Usan, Miri and Marudi (Miri), as well as the entire Limbang division.
In Sabah, warnings have been issued covering all areas in the Interior and West Coast, as well as Tongod and Telupid (Sandakan) and Kota Marudu (Kudat). — Bernama

Title: Sabah doesn’t just need films, it needs a film culture, says award-winning filmmaker Nadira Ilana amid industry brain drain
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Award-winning Malaysian filmmaker Nadira Ilana (pic) said Sabah’s creative industry was deliberately set back by the National Cultural Policy of 1971, stripping Sabahans of the cultural and linguistic freedoms needed to build a thriving arts sector.
“The creative industries in Sabah was ‘sabotaged’. We actually did have a creative industry, the beginnings of it, from the ’50s when the BBC introduced technologies like radio and cameras.
Sabahans were making our own documentaries in the 1970s,” she said at the Daily Express Creative Minds Forum 2026, to spotlight and celebrate the voices shaping Sabah’s creative landscape.
The event was held at Oitom restaurant and moderated by Daily Express Senior Journalist Ricardo Unto, himself an award-winning independent filmmaker.
Nadira said the 1971 policy, which centred national culture and language around the Malay identity, made it harder for Sabahan creatives to develop their craft in their own cultural context.
“Because of those national policies, it dictated whether or not I was able to speak Dusun, whether or not Sabahans could learn about our own history and cultural heritage. And it made things very challenging for a lot of Sabah creatives,” she said.
Nadira, who has been in the film industry for 16 years, said many talented Sabahans were forced to leave the State to develop their skills, contributing to a serious brain drain.
“The landscape is now shifting,” she said, crediting years of lobbying by Sabahan arts advocates for helping secure a dedicated ministerial portfolio for the creative economy.
She told the forum that growing Sabahan film audiences required more than just producing content.
“Sabah lacked the infrastructure to distribute, market and archive films, thus needs a film culture to match its growing output.
“We need to have a film culture along with the industry. We need to watch each other’s Sabahan films,” she said.
Nadira pointed to emerging talent as evidence that Sabah’s filmmakers were already gaining international recognition, citing directors Putri Purnama Sugua from Sandakan and Ekin Kee Charles, whose experimental video had been screened in Taiwan and beyond.
She said most universities in Sabah were not screening local films, meaning young filmmakers had little awareness of the work being produced in their own State.
“This requires community organising and other platforms. If we want to grow Sabahan film audiences, we need to watch each other’s Sabahan films,” said Nadira who leads Cinebah, a small film club she founded to screen Sabahan works and build awareness of local cinema. — Daily Express

Title: Where’s that umbrella: Thunderstorms, heavy rainfall expected all over Malaysia till up to 6pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in almost all states apart from Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya until 6pm today.
MetMalaysia in its warning at 2.25 pm said the weather conditions are expected to occur throughout the states of Perak, Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
In Kedah, the areas affected are Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu, while in Penang it includes Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan.
The weather conditions are also predicted to occur in Terengganu involving Dungun and Kemaman, while in Pahang it involves Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, Maran, Kuantan, Pekan and Rompin.
In Negeri Sembilan, it involves the areas of Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah and Rembau, while in Johor it includes Kluang, Mersing and Kota Tinggi.
For Sarawak, the areas predicted to be affected are Sarikei (Julau), Sibu (Sibu and Selangau), Mukah (Matu), Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Telang Usan) and Limbang (Lawas).
Meanwhile, in Sabah, warnings have been issued for the Interior (Sipitang, Tenom, Beaufort, Keningau and Tambunan), West Coast, Tawau (Tawau, Kunak and Lahad Datu) and Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan and Sandakan). — Bernama

Title: ‘Good luck’: Khaled responds to former Umno figures joining PAS
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin responded with a terse “good luck” when asked about the decision by several former party figures to join PAS.
Speaking after a working visit to the 4th Royal Artillery Regiment at Segamat Camp today, Khaled, who is also defence minister, said former minister Datuk Mohd Zaid Ibrahim had left Umno long ago and that his decision to join PAS was not unexpected.
He suggested Zaid, who previously served as Kelantan Umno deputy chairman, was seeking a new political platform.
“Good luck,” Khaled said, as reported by
Berita Harian
.
Earlier, PAS secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan announced the admission of 15 individuals during a ceramah held ahead of the Negeri Sembilan state election campaign in Seremban.
Among those introduced were Zaid; former Negeri Sembilan executive councillor and former Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Mohamad Rafie Abdul Malek; Rahimi Osman, a former private secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim; and former Perak executive councillor and former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman.
Takiyuddin said the group also included former youth leaders, civil servants and professionals from various backgrounds.

Title: Six Madani recreational parks in Penang to be completed by year-end
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 14 — Six Madani recreational parks in Penang are expected to be completed by the end of this year, expanding access to quality recreational facilities and enhancing the quality of life for residents in both urban and suburban areas.
State Local Government, Town and Country Planning Committee chairman Jason H’ng Mooi Lye said three projects under the Penang Island City Council (MBPP) have commenced implementation, while another three under the Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) are in the final stage of the quotation process.
“The three projects under MBPP are the upgrading of the Jalan Cheeseman neighbourhood park in Jelutong, Jalan Kampung Bukit neighbourhood park in Bayan Lepas, and Lengkok Merbah 3 neighbourhood park, also in Bayan Lepas.
“The implementation period for the Jalan Cheeseman project is from April 16 to Oct 15, while the two projects in Bayan Lepas are scheduled from June 5 to November 4,” he told Bernama recently.
For Seberang Perai, he said the projects involve landscape upgrading works at open spaces in Lorong Pauh 2, Taman Pauh and Jalan Kelisa Emas 4, Taman Kelisa in Seberang Perai Tengah, as well as Lorong Belatuk 3/Pekeliling Belatuk, Taman Belatuk in Nibong Tebal, Seberang Perai Selatan.
H’ng said the quotation advertisement process for the three projects was from May 19 to June 8, and they are currently at the document opening stage by the Quotation Committee before being tabled at the Quotation Committee Meeting in July.
“Site works will commence in the third week of July, and all three projects are expected to be completed by December,” he said.
He added that the selection of Madani park locations in Penang was evenly distributed between the island and Seberang Perai, with three projects allocated to each.
With a total allocation of RM2.5 million from the Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT), H’ng said project costs were determined based on the needs and scope of work at each location rather than being distributed equally.
The cost of projects under MBPP is RM412,580 for Jalan Cheeseman neighbourhood park, RM320,320 for Jalan Kampung Bukit, and RM381,100.50 for Lengkok Merbah 3, while the three projects under MBSP are each estimated at RM500,000.
H’ng said the Madani recreational parks are developed based on an inclusive concept that benefits all segments of society, regardless of age or physical ability.
Facilities to be provided include disability-friendly benches, children’s playground equipment, dedicated pathways and ramps for persons with disabilities, senior-friendly park benches, outdoor gyms, lighting systems, and closed-circuit television (CCTV) to enhance user safety.
He said each selected location also underwent engagement sessions with elected representatives, councillors, and Village Community Management Councils (MPKK) to ensure the facilities meet the actual needs of residents.
“The construction and upgrading of these recreational parks not only provide more comfortable and safer spaces for leisure activities, but also help strengthen social interaction, community wellbeing, and the quality of life of residents, especially in high-density areas,” he said.
On Jan 17, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming announced an allocation for the construction of six Madani recreational parks in Penang as part of efforts to enhance recreational facilities and community wellbeing.
The Madani recreational park development is one of KPKT’s key initiatives to bring recreational facilities closer to the people. Since its launch in 2024, the ministry has exceeded its annual target of building 100 parks. — Bernama

Title: Police seek public’s help to trace missing tahfiz student in Ampang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police are seeking the public’s assistance to trace a 15-year-old boy who was reported missing after he was believed to have left a tahfiz school in Ulu Kelang, Ampang, on Friday.
Ampang Jaya Police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said teachers at the school discovered that Muhammad Daniel Azizan Abdullah was missing and contacted his mother at 8.15 pm on Friday.
He said the teenager is approximately 175 centimetres tall, weighs 65 kilogrammes and has a fair complexion.
